text:
Statistical heterogeneity remains a significant challenge in the field of federated learning. The non-IID data distribution in the clients results in the poor generalization performance of the global shared model. In this paper, we propose a new FL algorithm named Federated Learning with Adaptive Local Aggregation (FedALA). In the proposed algorithm, the shared model is adapted for each client by a new Adaptive Local Aggregation (ALA) module to achieve better personalization. In each round, after the global model is downloaded by the client, the ALA module is activated to learn the optimal weight for the local optimization objective of the client, therefore achieving local adaptability. After the local update and uploading, the aggregation at the server side is also improved in order to enhance the diversity of the model updates.

We evaluate the performance of FedALA on five computer vision and natural language processing benchmarks. Fig. 6 and Fig. 7 show that FedALA surpasses all eleven baseline methods by at most 3.27% absolute improvement in test accuracy. Also, the ALA module can be used as a drop-in replacement for other popular federated learning methods to achieve at most 24.19% accuracy improvement. Our code is available at https://github.com/TsingZ0/FedALA.
